title: Elslack Estate, North Yorkshire
body: "If you’re interested in diversifying your business, read our case study for Elslack Estate, North Yorkshire which shows how woodland can increase land value and generate income for the landowner, whilst also providing environmental benefits such as habitat creation and natural flood management.  Key facts   site: Rectory Allotment, Elslack, North Yorkshire size: 45 hectares, with 63,000 trees type: mixed conifer and broadleaved woodland species: Sitka spruce, Norway spruce, Douglas fir, mixed native broadleaves including oak, birch and hazel agent: Edwin Thompson LLP, Carlisle grants: Woodland Creation Planning Grant (WCPG), Countryside Stewardship Woodland Creation Grant (CS WCG)  "
